expectations.text: Names code-simplifier as a dedicated subagent launched after implementation to find refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: 'Step 1: Launch code-simplifier subagent. When: Immediately after implementation is complete.' Focus: 'identifying refactoring opportunities — duplication between the cache integration points, over-engineered abstraction in the cache key construction, or any logic that can be flattened without changing behavior.'

expectations.text: Names one or more code-reviewer subagents launched for quality review, each with a stated focus (e.g. simplicity, correctness, conventions).
expectations.evidence: Three named subagents with distinct focuses: code-reviewer-1 — Correctness (cache invalidation, thread safety, error handling, TTL edge cases); code-reviewer-2 — Conventions (naming patterns, abstraction match, import organization, type annotation style); code-reviewer-3 — Simplicity (smallest, most direct implementation).

expectations.text: Places the code-simplifier pass before or alongside the code-reviewer pass, not after it.
expectations.evidence: 'The code-simplifier always runs first, before code review begins.' Table row: 'code-simplifier runs before code-reviewer | Source in skill: listed as Action 1 in Phase 6'. Step 2 states: 'After code-simplifier returns findings. Apply obvious improvements, then launch reviewers against the updated code.'

expectations.text: Gives each subagent a concrete focus description, not just a generic 'review the code' instruction.
expectations.evidence: Each subagent receives a quoted instruction block with specific file names and specific concerns. code-simplifier: 'Focus exclusively on identifying refactoring opportunities — duplication between the cache integration points...' Reviewers: each has a named focus area with concrete sub-questions (e.g., 'are stale entries evicted properly?', 'thread safety if these services share state').

expectations.text: Does not treat refactoring and quality review as a single unified step performed by one agent.
expectations.evidence: Table row explicitly: 'Refactoring and quality review are not the same step | code-simplifier = refactoring opportunities; code-reviewer = quality review — distinct mandates'. Steps 1 and 2 are sequenced and described separately.
